skill.name: doc-writer
skill.description: Create or update numbered task docs using repo context, DOC_TEMPLATE.md, and naming conventions.
skill.summary: Create or update numbered task docs using repo context, DOC_TEMPLATE.md, and naming conventions.
skill.author: 0xhiroki

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported shell execution and weak cryptography patterns, but the reviewed lines are Markdown code spans and prose, not executable code. The skill has a low residual risk because it instructs the assistant to read and write repository documentation files and optionally run configured validators.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Documentation Changes
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to create or update Markdown files in repository documentation directories. This is expected behavior for a documentation skill, but users should review generated files before commit.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly require generating or refreshing documentation files and saving them under documentation directories. This is a clear filesystem modification behavior, but it matches the declared purpose.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported shell backtick and weak cryptography findings are not executable code. The flagged text consists of Markdown code spans, file names, directory names, and a plain description.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is a short Markdown instruction file with no Ruby, shell script, or cryptographic implementation. The flagged backticks are Markdown formatting, not command substitution.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose task context when plans and completed work use different formats. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create consistent numbered task documents from repository context.
actual_capabilities: Reads docs/DOC_TEMPLATE.md to follow the repository document structure.
Creates numbered planned task documents under docs/planned/.
Creates numbered completed task documents under docs/completed/.
Uses PLAN.md, AGENTS.md, and existing task docs for context.
Checks numbering conventions before selecting a new two-digit prefix.
Records validation commands when documentation changes require checks.
limitations: Requires a repository that uses the expected docs/DOC_TEMPLATE.md structure.
Does not define a universal documentation format for unrelated projects.
Cannot verify business acceptance criteria without accurate repository context.
Validator execution depends on tools configured in the target repository.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Engineering Task
use_cases.description: Create a numbered planned task document with objectives, dependencies, steps, validation, and completion criteria.

use_cases.title: Document Completed Work
use_cases.description: Convert finished implementation context into a completed task record that follows the repository template.

use_cases.title: Standardize Task Notes
use_cases.description: Refresh older task notes so they match current template sections and repository naming conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Task Doc
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new planned task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the doc-writer skill to create a planned task document for adding user profile validation. Follow docs/DOC_TEMPLATE.md and choose the next available number.

prompt_templates.title: Update an Existing Task Doc
prompt_templates.scenario: Refresh a draft using current repository context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the doc-writer skill to update docs/planned/12-api-cleanup.md. Compare it with PLAN.md, AGENTS.md, and docs/DOC_TEMPLATE.md.

prompt_templates.title: Write a Completed Task Record
prompt_templates.scenario: Capture implementation details after work is finished.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the doc-writer skill to create a completed task document for the caching update. Include validation commands that were run and note any follow-up work.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Task Numbering and Structure
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for consistency across task docs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the doc-writer skill to review planned and completed task docs, identify numbering gaps or template drift, and update the requested task file only.

output_examples.input: Create a planned task document for improving search filters.
output_examples.output: A new planned task document with a unique two-digit prefix, template sections, implementation steps, validation guidance, and follow-up notes.

output_examples.input: Refresh an existing completed task note after tests changed.
output_examples.output: An updated completed task record that reflects the latest validation commands, repository paths, and completion criteria.

output_examples.input: Check whether a proposed task document follows the template.
output_examples.output: A concise review of missing sections, numbering issues, outdated references, and specific edits needed for compliance.

best_practices: Provide the task goal, affected files, and expected status before asking for a document.
Review generated docs for project-specific acceptance criteria before merging.
Run configured Markdown or repository validators when documentation changes require them.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to generate unrelated prose outside the repository task format.
Do not ask it to duplicate large source excerpts into task documents.
Do not skip review of numbering when creating a new task file.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates or updates numbered Markdown task documents for planned and completed repository work.

faq.question: Which template does it follow?
faq.answer: It follows docs/DOC_TEMPLATE.md when that file exists in the target repository.

faq.question: Can it choose the next task number?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks existing task documents and selects a unique two-digit prefix.

faq.question: Does it modify source code?
faq.answer: The skill is intended for documentation files. Source code changes are outside its stated purpose.

faq.question: Can it run validation commands?
faq.answer: It can run configured validators when documentation changes require checks, then record the commands used.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: It does not request network access, but it reads repository context. Review generated documentation before publishing.
